entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: An important leguminous allotetraploid crop cultivated in tropical and subtropical regions

entities.name: TROPICAL AND SUBTROPICAL REGIONS
entities.description: Regions where peanut is widely cultivated

entities.name: ALLOTETRAPLOID
entities.description: A genomic state characterized by having two sets of chromosomes from two different species

entities.name: A SUBGENOME
entities.description: One of the two subgenomes of the peanut genome

entities.name: B SUBGENOME
entities.description: One of the two subgenomes of the peanut genome

entities.name: ARACHIS DURANENSIS
entities.description: A diploid progenitor of the peanut A-genome

entities.name: ARACHIS IPAENSIS
entities.description: A diploid progenitor of the peanut B-genome

entities.name: GENOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of determining the complete DNA sequence of an organism's genome

entities.name: GENE FAMILY ANALYSIS
entities.description: A method used to identify and analyze groups of related genes

entities.name: FAR1 FAMILY
entities.description: A gene family potentially involved in regulating peanut fruit development

entities.name: FRUIT DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The biological process of fruit growth and maturation in peanut

entities.name: GENOMIC EVOLUTIONARY ANALYSES
entities.description: Analyses used to estimate divergence times and genomic events like duplication

entities.name: WHOLE-GENOME DUPLICATION
entities.description: An event where an organism's entire set of chromosomes is doubled

entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: Glycine max, a species from which Arachis ipaensis diverged

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to resist disease, with related genes identified in A. ipaensis

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia, with candidate genes identified in A. ipaensis

entities.name: NODULE DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The formation of root nodules for nitrogen fixation, regulated by homologous genes in A. ipaensis and A. duranensis

entities.name: DROUGHT ADAPTATION
entities.description: The ability of a plant to survive and thrive under water-limited conditions

entities.name: OIL BIOSYNTHESIS
entities.description: The metabolic pathway responsible for the production of oils

entities.name: FATTY ACID SYNTHESIS
entities.description: A specific process within oil biosynthesis

entities.name: TRIACYLGLYCEROL SYNTHESIS
entities.description: A specific process within oil biosynthesis

entities.name: FAD2
entities.description: A gene involved in oil biosynthesis found in both A. ipaensis and A. hypogaea
